base-domain/pom.xml.general_idea: Migrate Maven compiler source and target properties from Java 17 to Java 21 to align with the new JDK version.
base-domain/pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project would compile against Java 17 APIs, failing to utilize Java 21 features or potentially causing runtime incompatibilities if Java 21-specific libraries are used.

order-service/pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Spring Boot parent from 2.6.2 to 3.1.4 to align with Java 21 and modern Spring ecosystem.
order-service/pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 2.x does not support Java 21 as a baseline; upgrading is required to compile and run on JDK 21.

order-service/pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property from 17 to 21 to target the new JDK.
order-service/pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the build targets Java 17, which may not leverage Java 21 features or may fail if strict source/target checks are enabled for JDK 21.

order-service/pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade springdoc-openapi-ui from 1.5.13 to 1.7.0 to ensure compatibility with Spring Boot 3.
order-service/pom.xml.why_exists: Older versions of springdoc-openapi-ui are not compatible with Spring Boot 3 / Jakarta EE namespace changes.

payment-service/pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Spring Boot parent version to 3.1.4 to align with Java 21 and modern Spring ecosystem.
payment-service/pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 2.x does not support Java 21 as a baseline; upgrading is required to compile and run on JDK 21.

payment-service/pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property to 21 to target the new JDK.
payment-service/pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project would still target Java 17, failing to utilize Java 21 features or potentially causing compatibility issues with libraries requiring Java 21.

payment-service/src/main/java/pl/piomin/.general_idea: Replace javax.annotation with jakarta.annotation to align with Jakarta EE 9+ namespace requirements in Spring Boot 3.
payment-service/src/main/java/pl/piomin/.why_exists: Compile error: package javax.annotation does not exist in Java 21 / Spring Boot 3 environments which use the jakarta namespace.

payment-service/src/main/java/pl/piomin/.general_idea: Migrate JPA entity annotations from the legacy javax.persistence package to the Jakarta EE 9+ jakarta.persistence package.
payment-service/src/main/java/pl/piomin/.why_exists: The javax.persistence package is removed in Jakarta EE 9+ (Spring Boot 3.x / Java 21 ecosystem), causing a compile-time 'package does not exist' error.

stock-service/pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Spring Boot parent version to 3.1.4 to align with Java 21 and modern Spring ecosystem.
stock-service/pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 2.x does not support Java 21; upgrading to 3.x is required for compatibility.

stock-service/pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property to 21 to target the new JDK.
stock-service/pom.xml.why_exists: The project must compile against Java 21 features and bytecode version.

stock-service/src/main/java/pl/piomin/st.general_idea: Migrate javax.annotation.PostConstruct import to jakarta.annotation.PostConstruct to align with Jakarta EE namespace changes in Spring Boot 3.
stock-service/src/main/java/pl/piomin/st.why_exists: The javax.annotation package is no longer available in the default classpath of Spring Boot 3 / Jakarta EE 9+ applications, causing a compile error for the missing import.

stock-service/src/main/java/pl/piomin/st.general_idea: Migrate JPA annotations from javax.persistence to jakarta.persistence to align with Jakarta EE 9+ standards required by Spring Boot 3.
stock-service/src/main/java/pl/piomin/st.why_exists: Compilation failure: the javax.persistence package is removed in Jakarta EE 9+ and Spring Boot 3, causing 'package javax.persistence does not exist' errors.
